key_insight: FHRFormer uses a self-supervised masked transformer autoencoder to reconstruct missing fetal heart rate segments while preserving temporal and frequency structure for inpainting and forecasting.
engineering_relevance: The method can improve reliability of wearable fetal monitoring by reducing the impact of signal dropouts and enabling stronger downstream risk detection models.
method: Masks missing FHR segments during training and learns to reconstruct them.
Combines local temporal patterns with frequency-domain information in a transformer-based autoencoder.
Supports both signal inpainting and short-term forecasting from incomplete time series.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports robustness across varying missing-data durations and suitability for retrospective datasets used to build AI-based risk algorithms.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and diversity of training data, and real-world wearable deployment must handle frequent motion-induced artifact and sensor displacement.
paper_title: FHRFormer: A Self-Supervised Masked Transformer Framework for Fetal Heart Rate Time-Series Inpainting and Forecasting
